data.title: Waaree Energies stock zooms 7%, extends rally for five days as firm bags new order
data.description:
Waaree Energies stock has gained almost 16 percent in the past week.

Waaree Energies shares rallied as much as 7.2 percent to Rs 3,185 on December 10, extending its remarkable rally to five trading sessions in a row after it secured a new order to supply up to 1 GW of solar modules.

The order comes from a prominent renewable energy company in India, with deliveries planned for FY25 and FY26, as per the company’s regulatory filing. Waaree Energies, however, didn't disclose the monetary value of the order.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Waaree Energies, India’s leading solar PV module manufacturer, boasts an installed capacity of 12 GW across five facilities in Gujarat and Uttar Pradesh, spanning over 143 acres. Beyond manufacturing, the company is actively involved in solar project development and electricity sales.

In the second quarter, Waaree reported a 14.77 percent rise in consolidated net profit to Rs 361.65 crore, compared to Rs 315.09 crore in the same period last year. Revenue from operations saw a modest 1.04 percent year-on-year growth, totalling Rs 3,574.38 crore for the quarter ending September 2024.

Shares of Waaree Energies made their stock market debut on October 28, listing at Rs 2,500 apiece, a massive premium of 66.3 percent over the issue price of Rs 1,503 per share on the National Stock Exchange (NSE).

The Rs 4,321-crore IPO witnessed strong demand with the offer being oversubscribed 76.34 times. Investors bid for 160.91 crore shares, far exceeding the 2.1 crore shares on offer, according to exchange data.

At about 11:45, shares of the company were trading at Rs 3,126, higher by 5.2 percent from the last close on the NSE. Waaree Energies stock has gained almost 16 percent in the past week.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: CE Infosystems shares snap 4-day winning streak, slump 6% as profit booking kicks in
data.description:
Mapmyindia shares surged close to 23 percent in the past four days.

Shares of the owners of MapmyIndia--CE Info Systems tanked 6 percent on December 10 as investors rushed to take home partial profits after the recent bull run in the stock. With today's fall, the stock also snapped a four day winning streak wherein it clocked in close to 23 percent gains.

Despite the four-day rally in the stock, most of the gains were seen in the previous session, following the company's reversal of its decision to invest in the new consumer-facing business being established by CMD Rakesh Verma's son, CEO Rohan Verma. The board made this decision after receiving feedback from investors, which urged the company to forego plans of any equity or debt investment in the proposed new company.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 11.31 am, shares of CE Infosystems were trading at Rs 1,796.10 on the NSE. The sharp decline in the share price was also triggered by a spike in trading volumes in the counter. As much as eight lakh shares of the company changed hands on the exchanges so far, already higher than the one-month daily traded average of five lakh shares.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

Rohan Verma, the chief executive officer of MapmyIndia and son of founder chairman, Rakesh Verma, is leaving the company to form a new entity focusing on consumer business (B2C) in digital maps, geospatial products and platforms.

MapmyIndia proposed to fund the new venture, and on November 29, approved an investment of Rs 10 lakh for a 10 percent stake and an additional Rs 35 crore in Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) in a new company being set up by Rohan Verma. This decision sparked backlash from minority shareholders, as it raised concerns about its impact on the parent company's prospects in the high-growth B2C sector, leading to a sharp decline in the share price below the listing price. In response, the management clarified that the new venture would be funded by Rohan Verma's own funds.

The decision to hive off certain consumer-facing mapping and navigation businesses, which offered high-growth potential, has also unsettled some minority investors and analysts, also adding to the sharp decline today.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Golden era for Indian capital markets; over a dozen stocks to ride the wave, says Motilal Oswal report
data.description:
Stocks of asset management companies (AMCs), exchanges, brokers, wealth managers, and other intermediaries are well-positioned to capitalise on these emerging trends, says the report.

The remarkable growth of the Indian capital market in the past five years marks the beginning of a sustained, multi-year structural uptrend, fuelled by favourable demographic trends, says a latest report by Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

Stocks of asset management companies (AMCs), exchanges, brokers, wealth managers, and other intermediaries are well-positioned to capitalise on these emerging trends, it adds. HDFC AMC, Nippon AMC, Angel One, BSE, Nuvama, and CAMS among others are the top picks of the domestic broking major.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

“Digital enablers such as E-KYC, UPI, and Account Aggregation have played a key role in facilitating this growth. Regulatory reforms have further strengthened the ecosystem, enhancing transparency and security for investors,” stated the report while highlighting demographic trends including more individuals entering the workforce and contributing to the expansion of the middle class.

“Over the next decade, the demographic dividend will accelerate, with over 100 million people joining the workforce and about 100 million households entering the middle-income class. The HNI and UHNI segments are also clocking 12% CAGR and are likely to sustain this momentum,” it added.

The domestic broking firm is of the view that the entire ecosystem of capital market -- AMCs, brokers, exchanges, intermediaries, and wealth managers -- will see sustained growth in revenue (17-45% CAGR over FY24-27).

Further, it believes that fixed cost nature will drive operating leverage for all segments, resulting in superior profit growth (12-75% CAGR over FY24-27). High cash generation, healthy dividend payouts, and superior RoEs bolster our view in the entire capital market space, stated the report.

It, however, also highlighted that the demat penetration level in India is a low 12% compared to 62% for USA; even the turnover velocity at around 70% is quite less than NASDAQ’s 115%. Further, the MF AUM-to-GDP ratio at 17% is lagging far behind than the global average of 65%.

In terms of stock picks, it prefers HDFC AMC and Nippon AMC among listed mutual funds, while Angel One continues to be its top pick in the broking space. Among exchanges, the domestic broking major likes BSE and among Wealth Managers, it likes Nuvama and 360 One WAM.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Further, among Intermediaries, it prefers CAMS and has a buy recommendation UTI AMC and Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC and neutral rating on MCX, Anand Rathi, KFin, Prudent and CDSL.

Here are some of the recommendations and valuation summary

HDFC AMC BUY (CMP Rs 4,365; Target Rs 5,200 (+19%))

Strong fund performance for three years leading to 150bp market share gains in equity segment in two years. HDFC Bank channel to pick up further momentum as branch network expands deeper and incremental resources are deployed. Expect FY24-27 AUM/revenue/core PAT CAGR of 23%/21%/23%. Trades at FY26E P/E of 32x

ABSL AMC BUY (CMP Rs 848 Target Rs 1,100 (+30%))

Recent pick up in fund performance should translate into market share gains in the medium term; strong focus is also on deepening distribution. Aims to increase offerings in the alternate space; with teams in place, scale is expected in the near term. Expect a CAGR of 18%/17%/20% in AUM/revenue/core PAT over FY24-27. Trades at FY26E P/E of 22x

Angel One BUY (CMP Rs 3,292 Target Rs 4,200 (+28%))

Rs 15 billion fundraise, new features on app, and a new investment tool to drive near-term broking business. Investments in AMC, wealth management, and the AP channel will increase share of non-broking revenue to 30% in four years. Expect FY24-27 revenue/PAT CAGR of 24%/25%. Trades at FY26E P/E of 17x

BSE BUY (CMP Rs 5,396 Target Rs 6,500 (+20%))

Sensex/Bankex have gained market share in the options segment, with an overall notional/premium T/O market share of 25%+/10%. Co-location services, Star MF, and new products such as power futures and gold can be long-term drivers. Expect FY24-27 revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR of 45%/74%/74%. Trades at FY26E P/E of 42x

360 ONE WAM BUY (CMP Rs 1,120 Target Rs 1,350 (+21%))

Fortifying its leadership in the UHNI segment through geographical expansion; AMC is well poised for sustained growth in alternate space. Investments in the HNI segment and global WM space to fructify over the medium term. Expect FY24-27 ARR AUM/revenue/ PAT CAGR of 24%/ 20%/23%. Trades at FY26E P/E of 34x

CAMS BUY (CMP Rs 5,072 Target Rs 6,000 (+18%))

Largest MF RTA with around 68% AUM market share in a duopoly market, 10 out of top 15 AMCs are customers of CAMS, a perfect proxy for MF growth. CamsPay, AIF/PMS RTA, and Insurance Repository will drive non-MF share to 20%+ in two years from 13% currently. Expect FY24-27 revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR of 20%/24%/26%. Trades at FY26E P/E of 42x.

data.title: RBI reviewing Tata Sons' IPO exemption application, examining request to surrender registration certificate: CNBC-TV18
data.description:
The NBFC (upper layer) status for Tata Sons stems from its bank borrowings, as per RBI norms.

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has said that Tata Sons' application seeking exemption from a mandatory Initial Public Offering (IPO) is under review, reported CNBC-TV18. In response to a Right to Information (RTI) application dated November 14, 2024, the central bank also said that Tata Sons' request to surrender its Certificate of Registration (COR) as a Core Investment Company (CIC) is still under examination, according to the report.

Tata Sons, the holding company of the Tata Group, was classified as an upper-layer non-banking financial company (NBFC-UL) in September 2022 under RBI's scale-based regulations. This classification mandates listing on stock exchanges within three years, giving Tata Sons a deadline of September 2025.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The NBFC-UL status stems from its bank borrowings, as per RBI norms. Listing is a regulatory requirement for upper-layer NBFCs to enhance governance and transparency in systemically important entities.

Efforts to avoid mandatory listing

According to an August 2024 report by Moneycontrol, Tata Sons has been in discussions with the RBI since December 2023 to avoid the IPO mandate. The company has reportedly committed to significant debt reduction as part of its efforts to exit the NBFC-UL classification.

Tata Sons has already taken substantial steps to pare its debt, including allocating Rs 9,000 crore from the sale of a 0.6 percent stake in its flagship subsidiary, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), earlier this year. It has also considered selling additional stakes in other subsidiaries to accelerate debt reduction. As of January 31, ICRA Ratings estimated Tata Sons' total debt at Rs 15,173 crore.

The company aims to be debt-free by the end of FY25, potentially exempting it from the NBFC-UL classification and the subsequent IPO requirement, according to sources cited by Moneycontrol.

data.title: Tiger Logistics board approves plan to raise up to Rs 400 cr, shares gain
data.description:
Tiger Logistics board approves plan to raise up to Rs 400 cr

Shares of Tiger Logistics (India) Ltd rallied in today's trading session after the company announced that its board has approved a plan to raise up to Rs 400 crore through a preferential issue.

In a regulatory filing, the company stated that it is considering multiple financial instruments, including equity shares, to secure the proposed funds.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

“To support our business growth plans, the board has granted in-principle approval to raise funds aggregating up to Rs 400 crore. This will be done through the issuance of equity shares, convertible instruments, or other permissible securities in one or more tranches via preferential issue,” the company said in its statement.

Tiger Logistics (India) Ltd is a prominent global logistics firm specialising in the domestic and international import and export of cargo projects.

data.title: Sensex, Nifty remain flat ahead of key inflation data; IT, financial stocks gain
data.description:
Among sectoral indices, Nifty IT and Nifty Bank led gains, up 0.8 percent and 0.1 percent, respectively.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

The Sensex and Nifty were trading marginally higher by noon on December 10, buoyed by gains in IT and financial services stocks. This followed a muted start for the second consecutive session, as traders treaded cautiously ahead of US and India CPI data due later this week.

At 11:40 AM, the Sensex was up 136 points or 0.2 percent at 81,644, and the Nifty was up 35 points or 0.1 percent at 24,653. About 1,815 shares advanced, 1,542 shares declined, and 104 shares remained unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"Market is likely to move in a narrow consolidation pattern in the near-term. There are no major triggers that can push the market into a new bull orbit. Also, there are no major triggers that can cause a deep correction from the current levels," said V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services.

Investors also digested the surprise appointment of Sanjay Malhotra as the new Reserve Bank of India Governor on December 9. Bank of America highlighted the challenging backdrop he faces, citing a sharper-than-expected slowdown in growth, near-term inflation volatility, and the need to stabilise the rupee.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

Additionally, expectations of Chinese stimulus stirred market interest. Beijing announced plans to adopt "moderately loose monetary policies" next year and introduce steps to boost consumption, marking its first such policy shift in 14 years.

Commenting on these developments, market veteran Sunil Subramanian advised patience in reacting to China's measures. "This step by China could be a way to counter Trump's tariffs against the country. The last time such a measure was announced by China, the execution of it was quite underwhelming," he told Moneycontrol.

The domestic market has entered a consolidation phase, expected to persist until the release of key inflation metrics. The US CPI data is due on December 11, followed by India's CPI figures on December 12, ahead of the Federal Reserve meeting on December 17-18.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Among sectoral indices, Nifty IT and Nifty Bank led gains, up 0.8 percent and 0.1 percent, respectively. "Financials are resilient and moving up on good prospects and attractive valuations," Vijayakumar said adding that the resilience of banking and IT stocks is likely to continue for some more time since there are tailwinds that can support their up move.

Also Read | Change of guard at RBI corner office roils rupee, but boosts bond yields

In contrast, energy and auto stocks underperformed. Reliance Industries (RIL) and ONGC dragged the Nifty Oil and Gas index down by 0.2 percent. Nifty Auto extended its losses by 0.2 percent following weak November retail sales data for passenger and commercial vehicles.

Tata Motors defied the auto sector's trend, rising over 1 percent after announcing a price hike of up to 3 percent across its lineup, effective January.

Among individual stocks, LIC shares dropped nearly 3 percent following a decline in November premiums.

Broader market indices displayed mixed trends, with the domestically-focused BSE Smallcap flat and the BSE Midcap index rising 0.2 percent.

Also Read | NSE retains options trading edge after SEBI’s F&O overhaul

Sameet Chavan, Head of Technical and Derivative Research of Angel One identified the 24,750-24,800 range as a critical resistance zone for Nifty.  He said that a sustained these levels could open the door for a rise beyond the 25,000 mark. He expects Nifty 50 to find support at 24,400 in the event of any dips.

"Traders should monitor the range between 24,400 and 24,800, with a 'buy on dip and book profits at higher ranges' strategy during this consolidation phase," he added.

On the Nifty 50, top gainers included Shriram Finance, Bajaj Finserv, Infosys, Wipro, and Tata Steel, with gains of 1-3 percent. Meanwhile, Bharti Airtel, Dr. Reddy's Labs, Trent, Adani Ports, and Grasim were the top losers, declining around 1 percent each.

data.title: Motilal Oswal picks 'Bruised Bluechips' in latest wealth study, Raamdeo shares the rationale
data.description:
It will be tough to beat Nifty in the long run as 20-22 of the companies on the index are bluechips: Raamdeo Agrawal

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Motilal Oswal is releasing its latest Wealth Creation Study for 2024 on December 10, in which the broking and asset management company has talked about creating wealth through 'Bruised Bluechips', or quality companies that have not been delivering returns.

The underperformance of these bluechips could range from external factors to misallocation of capital, Raamdeo Agrawal told CNBC-TV18, but added that the 'asymmetric payoff' here is an huge opportunity to invest. "When these companies come back, they come back roaring," added  Raamdeo.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

What are Bruised Bluechips

The Nifty 50 index remains a key quotient of this framework, as the presence of bluechips in the index makes it 'tough' to beat Nifty returns in the long run.

Aside of the Nifty 50 names, Motilal Oswal has assigned the definition of companies generating 20% RoA over past ten years as a bluechip, which has qualified 107 companies as part of this framework of study.

How Bruised Bluechips were Assessed

-Companies that have fallen more than 50% from their five-year highs

-The reasons for the bruising of the company

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

-The shares are near the point of maximum pessimism

-The companies should have the competence to come back

-Healing triggers before finally deciding on buying, only after valuations get attractive

The Motilal Oswal study attempts to generate wealth from turnaround stories in the economy. "The best time to buy these names is when is are in a bit of trouble," Raamdeo said, adding that the pursuit for 'robust bluechips' always leads to stocks with very expensive valuations, hence Motilal Oswal developed this framework.

Market Outlook

Raamdeo Agrawal sounded optimistic about 2025, and said it will be a 'fantastic year'.

'Next year, even if FIIs invest $20-25 billion, we will have a party', Raamdeo added. The CY24 has seen an inflow of $60 billion from domestic investors, which cushioned the intense selling from foreign investors. He added that Centre's focus on capex will lead to capital goods, defence and railway plays continue to find a favour in 2025 as well.

data.title: MC exclusive: Ransomware attack targets Comtel data centre that services around 16 brokers
data.description:
Sources told Moneycontrol that in the evening hours of December 9, a ransomware attack was launched on the data centre that compromised the security of nearly 16 stock brokers and their clients.

Around 16 brokers, including big brokerage houses such as IIFL Securities, 5 Paisa and Axis Securities, have been affected by a ransomware attack that targeted Comtel, a data centre, according to sources.

The brokers were blocked by two of the three leading exchanges and would need to get a certificate from certified auditors to regain access to the exchanges, as per the laid-down norms of the market regulator, Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), the sources added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Comtel data centre houses stock broker's servers and other networking equipment. It provides various services, including rack space, power and bandwidth, and also rents out hardware.

The services are, usually, used by brokerages to better manage costs. Brokerages can either maintain their own data centres or use shared services as provided by Comtel. Moneycontrol has written to Comtel and brokerage houses for their responses. This article will be updated as and when they respond.

Also read: "Ignorance of law cannot be an excuse": SEBI fines Premier Polyfilm for related-party transactions

Sources told Moneycontrol that in the evening of December 9, a ransomware attack was launched on Comtel that compromised the security of nearly 16 stock brokers and their clients.

The threat may have infected a sister concern Symphony's multi-asset order management system (OMS), too. This may even have been the entry point for the data centre to be affected, added the sources. But if the attack was limited to the OMS, then the extent of the risk may have been limited to those clients whose orders are routed through this system. However, there was fear that with the data centre compromised the risk may have been wider.

According to the sources, it is feared that the details of the clients could have been accessed through the data centre and their order flow could have been compromised. Usually, this is the pattern of the order flow: from a client to a broker, from a broker to a data centre and from a data centre to the exchanges.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Following the ransomware attack, the sources said that the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) and National Stock Exchange (NSE) have closed down access for most of these brokers in line with the SEBI rules.

The norms stipulate that the brokers need to obtain certificates from Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA), who must unequivocally state that systems are free from any ransomware and are operating smoothly. Only on the basis of this certification, the exchanges allow brokers to operate, the sources added.
data.author: MoneyControl
